title: Mellow Salty Norwegian Salmon Tagine
description: A velvety Norwegian salmon tagine with a salty brine, caramelized onions, and fragrant herbs, served in a traditional tagine pot for a nostalgic Nordic experience.
instructions: Pat salmon fillets dry and season with sea salt, black pepper, dill, parsley, and lemon juice; set aside for 10 minutes.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in the tagine pot over medium heat, add sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add sliced carrots to the pot and cook for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Push the vegetables to the side of the pot, pour the remaining tablespoon of olive oil, and sear the salmon fillets skin side down for 2 minutes.
Flip the salmon, cover the tagine, and add 1 cup of water; then bake in the preheated oven for 15 minutes until the salmon flakes easily.
Remove the tagine from the oven, fluff the salmon with a fork, and drizzle with the remaining lemon juice.
Serve the salmon and vegetables hot, garnished with extra dill and parsley if desired.
